words.hint: Smallest unit of a chemical element
words.educational: An atom is the basic unit of matter consisting of protons, neutrons, and electrons. All elements are made of atoms.

words.hint: Capacity to do work or cause change
words.educational: Energy is the ability to do work. It exists in many forms including kinetic, potential, thermal, and chemical energy.

words.hint: Force that attracts objects toward each other
words.educational: Gravity is a fundamental force that attracts all objects with mass toward each other. It keeps planets in orbit around the sun.

words.hint: Basic unit of life in living organisms
words.educational: Cells are the smallest units of life. All living things are made of one or more cells that carry out life processes.
Oxygen is a chemical element essential for respiration in most living organisms. It makes up about 21% of Earth's atmosphere.
A planet is a large celestial body that orbits a star. Our solar system has eight planets including Earth.
Light is electromagnetic radiation that travels in waves. It moves at the fastest known speed in the universe.

words.hint: Liquid compound essential for all life
words.educational: Water (H2O) is a molecule made of hydrogen and oxygen. It's essential for all known forms of life on Earth.

words.hint: Push or pull that can change motion
words.educational: Force is an interaction that can change an object's motion. It's measured in Newtons and described by Newton's laws of motion.
A photon is a fundamental particle of light and all other electromagnetic radiation. It has no mass but carries energy.
